real_architecture_summary: Runtime registry consumers use project.spec_registry and project/domain/registry_loader.py against unified event/template/grammar specs. Search generation still uses older spec_validation facades for search YAMLs. Proposal, search, validation, promotion, blueprint/spec, and engine/live paths are split across different loaders and consistency checks.
top_invariants: Entry lag must be >= 1 everywhere to avoid same-bar leakage.
Compiled default template/lag resolution must match spec/ontology/templates/template_registry.yaml.
Runtime registry consistency checks must actually read the same spec surfaces as the authoritative CI checks.

findings.title: Compiled registry ignores the declared default entry-lag grid and falls back to (1, 2)
findings.evidence: DomainRegistry.default_entry_lags() still looks for template_param_grid_defaults, while the template registry declares defaults.param_grids.common.entry_lag_bars = [1, 2, 3].
findings.why_it_matters: Wildcard/default lag expansion under-enumerates the declared search space and drops lag 3.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Compare get_domain_registry().default_entry_lags() with get_domain_registry().template_defaults() and the YAML in template_registry.yaml.
findings.suggested_remediation: Read defaults.param_grids.common.entry_lag_bars (or support both keys during migration) and add a regression test for [1,2,3].

findings.title: Direct experiment-config path accepts zero-lag hypotheses that search validators classify as leakage
findings.evidence: validate_agent_request() only checks list size for entry_lags, not values, and build_experiment_plan() expands e2e_synth_small.yaml with entry_lag 0 even though validate_hypothesis_spec() rejects lag < 1.
findings.why_it_matters: The experiment-engine entrypoint can generate same-bar-leakage hypotheses that the core search validator would reject.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Load e2e_synth_small.yaml, call validate_agent_request(), then build_experiment_plan() and inspect the emitted entry_lag values.
findings.suggested_remediation: Enforce entry_lag >= 1 in experiment_engine_validators and normalize existing configs away from zero.

findings.title: Authoritative search-limit defaults advertise invalid zero-lag values that the proposal pipeline rejects
findings.evidence: search_limits.yaml declares defaults.entry_lags: [0,1,2], but proposal normalization and proposal-schema validation reject lag < 1.
findings.why_it_matters: The same registry file is presented as authoritative defaults but cannot survive the proposal path that consumes it.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Run _load_search_limit_defaults() against project/configs/registries/search_limits.yaml or compare the YAML with _normalize_entry_lags().
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove 0 from search_limits.yaml or change the downstream leakage contract; the mixed state is self-invalidating.

findings.title: carry_state=neutral is declared as allowed in one spec but rejected by the compiled runtime context map
findings.evidence: state_families.yaml allows neutral, but the compiled context map only loads funding_pos and funding_neg from spec/grammar/state_registry.yaml, and validation rejects neutral as unknown_context_mapping.
findings.why_it_matters: Proposal/search/runtime semantics for carry_state are internally contradictory.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Validate a HypothesisSpec using context={"carry_state":"neutral"} and compare with both state spec files.
findings.suggested_remediation: Add neutral to the compiled context map or remove it from the allowed-values surface.

findings.title: Experiment-engine registry consistency check is effectively unreachable because it reads the wrong registry shapes
findings.evidence: validate_registry_consistency() expects families/event_families keys in RegistryBundle-loaded config files, but those keys are not present, so the function returns early and never performs the claimed check. CI tests use different authoritative files under spec/grammar and spec/ontology/templates.
findings.why_it_matters: The runtime safeguard is a no-op, so drift can be caught in one test stack but never by the direct runtime path.
findings.suggested_remediation: Point the runtime check at the same authoritative spec files as the CI consistency test, or remove the dead runtime check.

findings.title: cost_profiles and conditioning_intersections are declared in search specs but have no runtime consumers
findings.evidence: The keys are present in search YAMLs, but repo search across project/research, project/spec_validation, project/spec_registry, and project/specs finds no runtime consumers.
findings.why_it_matters: Operator-facing knobs appear authoritative but currently do nothing.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Search the runtime code for cost_profiles and conditioning_intersections and compare with their YAML declarations.
findings.suggested_remediation: Implement consumers or reject/deprecate these unsupported keys in schema validation.

test_coverage_assessment: Spec loader and consistency tests exist, but they do not catch zero-lag experiment configs, compiled default-lag drift, the carry_state neutral mismatch, or the fact that runtime registry-consistency validation early-returns without checking anything.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: The CI registry-consistency test uses spec/ontology/templates and spec/grammar/family_registry, while the runtime check reads unrelated config shapes and is effectively a no-op.
search_limits.yaml advertises default zero-lag values, while proposal and search validation explicitly prohibit zero lag.
suggested_regression_tests: A direct experiment-engine zero-lag rejection test.
A context-label parity test between state_families.yaml and the compiled context map.
open_questions_or_unknowns: Whether mixed legacy/unified registry support is temporary cutover state or unintended long-term drift.
